items.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Linux Root Access Bug CVE-2026-31431 to KEV
items.summary: CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS 7.8) is a local privilege escalation flaw in the Linux kernel that allows an unprivileged local user to obtain root access. CISA has added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog confirming active exploitation in the wild.
items.why: Root-level LPE with confirmed active exploitation across Linux servers and cloud workloads makes this urgent patching priority for any Linux-based infrastructure.

items.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS (CVSS 9.1)
items.summary: Fortinet released emergency patches for CVE-2026-35616, a CVSS 9.1 improper access control vulnerability in FortiClient EMS. Attackers can bypass authentication through the API and escalate privileges. Active exploitation confirmed in the wild.
items.why: CVSS 9.1 with in-the-wild exploitation in widely deployed enterprise endpoint management software — unpatched instances give attackers full administrative control over managed endpoints.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours to Target European Military
items.summary: Russian state-sponsored APT28 (Fancy Bear) exploited a newly disclosed Microsoft Office 1-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-21509) within 24 hours of its public disclosure. The multi-stage campaign deploys stealthy malware with cloud-based C2 infrastructure against European military and government entities.
items.why: Russian APT exploiting 1-days within 24 hours of disclosure against NATO military targets signals dangerous acceleration in operational tempo and significant intelligence collection risk.

items.title: Iran's APT 'Screening Serpens' Deploys 6 New RAT Variants Targeting US, Israel, and UAE
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 tracks Iranian APT group Screening Serpens, which has dramatically intensified operations since February 2026, developing and deploying six new remote access Trojan variants in under three months. Targets span entities in the US, Israel, UAE, and two additional Middle Eastern countries.
items.why: Six new RAT variants in three months indicates rapid Iranian cyber capability development tied directly to regional conflict escalation — high-value espionage and disruption risk for Middle East and US defense sectors.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Attackers Hand Off Access in 22 Seconds, Median Dwell Time Rises to 14 Days
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report, based on 500,000+ hours of IR engagements, finds attackers are handing off compromised access internally in as little as 22 seconds. Exploits remain the #1 initial access vector for the sixth consecutive year. Threat actors are increasingly targeting backup infrastructure and identity services to force ransom payments and deny recovery.
items.why: Authoritative annual benchmark from Mandiant's global IR team — the 22-second internal handoff and systematic backup destruction are paradigm shifts that demand updated IR playbooks and recovery architecture.
